description: Row's rank by total_overpayment, descending (1 = highest).
Free-text Cost Plus catalog query string, "<drug> <strength> <form>", used to resolve this row via the crosswalk.
RxNorm Concept Unique Identifier this drug resolved to.
Unit NADAC prices this drug in: EA (each, e.g. tablet/capsule), ML (milliliter), or GM (gram). The canonical per-unit basis for every comparison on this row.
National Average Drug Acquisition Cost per unit -- the closest public proxy to true pharmacy acquisition cost. Not net of any rebate.
Cost Plus Drugs' price per unit: (acquisition_cost * markup + pharmacy_fee) / package_quantity.
costplus_per_unit minus nadac_per_unit -- Cost Plus's own margin over acquisition cost. NOT an overpayment figure; never summed into the savings totals.
Medicare Part D national average gross price per dosage unit for this drug's molecule (Tot_Spndng / Tot_Dsg_Unts), aggregated across every strength of the ingredient. Gross of manufacturer rebates.
source: CMS Medicare Part D Spending by Drug

description: Total Medicare Part D dosage units dispensed nationally for this drug's molecule (every strength combined) in the reporting year.
source: CMS Medicare Part D Spending by Drug

description: partd_per_unit minus costplus_per_unit, this row's own strength. Informational per-strength gap; never itself multiplied into a dollar total (see overpayment_partd).
Number of Cost Plus catalog strengths sharing this molecule's Part D national figure. 0 if this drug has no Part D match.

The highest costplus_per_unit among this molecule's strengths -- the single representative price used to dollarize Part D overpayment once per molecule (a conservative floor, since it minimizes the gap; no public data exists to weight by real strength-mix).

True only for the one row per molecule that carries the molecule's dollarized overpayment_partd; every other strength row of that molecule is False with overpayment_partd = 0, preventing double counting when summed.

Estimated annual Medicare Part D overpayment for this drug's molecule vs. Cost Plus, dollarized once per molecule (see is_partd_molecule_row). Floored at 0 -- a negative gap contributes 0, not a negative number.
National Medicaid gross reimbursement per unit from SDUD's national 'XX' rollup, aggregated across all NDCs matched to this rxcui.
Total national Medicaid units reimbursed for this drug's matched NDCs.
medicaid_per_unit minus costplus_per_unit.
gap_medicaid * medicaid_units, floored at 0 (a negative gap contributes 0, not a negative number).
overpayment_partd + overpayment_medicaid. The figure leaderboard.csv is sorted by.
Cost Plus's flat per-fill pharmacy fee, already folded into costplus_per_unit's formula. Kept as its own column for transparency.
Cost Plus's flat per-order shipping fee. Deliberately NOT part of costplus_per_unit (a per-order, not per-dose, cost).
Literal constant string "not public" on every row. True rebate-net prices are not public and are never estimated in this dataset.
Copy of nadac_pricing_unit -- the unit basis this row's comparisons are made on.

Literal constant string "token_overlap" on every row: every leaderboard row passed the crosswalk's token-overlap relevance check (see shared/crosswalk.py). Rows that failed this check never reach the leaderboard at all.

unit: percent (if best_confirmed_type=markup_pct or spread_pct) or USD (if spread_dollars)
description: Highest confirmed public markup/spread value found for this rxcui across every citation source. See best_confirmed_type for the unit.
Unit of best_confirmed_spread: markup_pct (% over NADAC acquisition cost), spread_pct (intermediary profit as % of total spend), or spread_dollars (flat $ per claim).
Citation string (source name + page number) for best_confirmed_spread.
example_value: Lewandowski v. Johnson & Johnson, ERISA Class Action Complaint (D.N.J. 1:24-cv-00671, filed Feb. 2024), p.44

description: Category of best_confirmed_source: federal_study (FTC interim reports -- industry-wide sample), state_disclosure (Maine MHDO -- mandated reporting), peer_reviewed (JAMA Mattingly et al. -- academic sample), or litigation (J&J/Wells Fargo ERISA complaints -- plaintiff-selected, not a representative sample). See METHODOLOGY.md's selection-bias caveat.
Every confirmed citation for this rxcui (not just the max that wins best_confirmed_spread), pipe-separated, each formatted 'value (source_type, source_name, p.page)', highest value first.
example_value: 6,391.86% (litigation, Lewandowski v. Johnson & Johnson..., p.44) | 1,727.73% (state_disclosure, Maine MHDO..., p.23)

description: nadac_per_unit * (1 + best_confirmed_spread/100). Only computed when best_confirmed_type == markup_pct (no defensible per-unit-price formula exists for spread_pct/spread_dollars). Labeled as an estimate, not a directly observed price.
Explanatory label for estimated_pbm_price_per_unit: "Estimated from confirmed markup % -- not a directly observed price". Blank when estimated_pbm_price_per_unit is blank.
example_value: Estimated from confirmed markup % -- not a directly observed price

description: Two-letter US state/territory postal code (SDUD's own state coding, includes DC and territories like PR).
RxNorm Concept Unique Identifier.
Cost Plus catalog drug_term for this drug (same value as leaderboard.csv's drug_term column).
Same definition as leaderboard.csv's costplus_per_unit.
This state's own Medicaid gross reimbursement per unit for this drug's matched NDCs (state-level SDUD, not the national XX rollup).
medicaid_per_unit minus costplus_per_unit, for this state.
This state's total Medicaid units reimbursed for this drug.

gap_medicaid * units_reimbursed for this state. UNLIKE leaderboard.csv, this is NOT floored at 0 -- a negative value here is a real, visible finding (Cost Plus priced above this state's Medicaid reimbursement). Only (state, drug) pairs with >0 reimbursed units appear at all; nothing is zero-filled.
Two-letter US state/territory postal code.

Sum of only the POSITIVE overpayment_medicaid rows for this state in leaderboard_by_state.csv (negative-gap drugs contribute $0, not a negative number, mirroring leaderboard.csv's national convention).
drug_name of this state's single highest-overpayment_medicaid drug.
overpayment_medicaid value for top_drug. 0.0 if the state has no drug with a positive gap.
Distinct rxcui count with reimbursed Medicaid units in this state (positive or negative gap, i.e. every drug that appears for this state in leaderboard_by_state.csv).

Shortest observed drug-name spelling across every source citing this drug -- a deterministic proxy for its plain ingredient name (different sources name the same molecule differently, e.g. FTC's 'Octreotide (Sandostatin) Injectable' vs. litigation's 'Octreotide Acetate'; both consolidate to one row).

Highest markup_pct confirmed by the Maine MHDO Prescription Drug Pricing and Transparency Report for this drug. Column present only because at least one gap drug is cited by this source; blank for rows this source doesn't cite.
source: Maine MHDO Prescription Drug Pricing and Transparency Report (2022, covering CY2021)

description: Highest markup_pct confirmed by the Lewandowski v. Johnson & Johnson ERISA litigation complaint for this drug.
source: Lewandowski v. Johnson & Johnson, ERISA Class Action Complaint (D.N.J. 1:24-cv-00671, filed Feb. 2024)

description: Highest markup_pct confirmed by the Navarro v. Wells Fargo & Co. ERISA litigation complaint for this drug.
source: Navarro v. Wells Fargo & Co., ERISA Class Action Complaint (D. Minn. 0:24-cv-03043, filed Jul. 2024)

description: Highest markup_pct confirmed by an FTC interim staff report for this drug.
Semicolon-separated list of every full source_name that cites this drug.
example_value: FTC Second Interim Staff Report (Jan 2025); Lewandowski v. Johnson & Johnson...

description: Page number(s), one per citing source, formatted '<page> (<source tag>)', slash-separated when more than one source cites the drug.
Cost Plus catalog query string for this row, "<drug> <strength> <form>".
Resolved RxNorm Concept Unique Identifier (a real, correctly-typed generic concept -- crosswalk succeeded).
RxNorm term type of the resolved concept: SCD (generic clinical drug) or GPCK (generic pack) -- both are 'real' dispensable types, not a crosswalk failure.

Number of NDCs RxNav returned for this rxcui. None of them have a current NADAC price -- this is why the row is in this file rather than leaderboard.csv (an external NADAC data-coverage gap, not a catalog gap or a crosswalk bug).

RxNorm Concept Unique Identifier this citation is matched to. Matching is by hand or by an explicit, documented disambiguation rule (sole leaderboard candidate for the ingredient, an explicit strength stated in the source, or the source's implied per-unit price within 10% of the leaderboard's NADAC for a candidate strength) -- never guessed. See METHODOLOGY.md.

Drug name as used for this citation -- either the leaderboard's own canonical drug_term (litigation/FTC rows, matched after the fact) or the original source document's own drug/manufacturer description (Maine/JAMA rows, which include NDC-level manufacturer detail).
NADAC price for this rxcui, copied from leaderboard.csv at the time this citation was matched.
Cost Plus price for this rxcui, copied from leaderboard.csv at the time this citation was matched.
The markup/spread figure exactly as stated in the source document -- never invented, interpolated, or estimated. See confirmed_spread_type for the unit.
Unit of confirmed_spread_value: markup_pct (% over NADAC), spread_pct (intermediary gross profit as % of spend), or spread_dollars (flat $ per claim).
Full name/citation of the report or court filing this figure was extracted from. See dataset/PROVENANCE.md for each source's URL and access date.
example_value: Lewandowski v. Johnson & Johnson, ERISA Class Action Complaint (D.N.J. 1:24-cv-00671, filed Feb. 2024)

description: Publication year of the source document.
Page number within the source document where this figure appears.
Short (under 15 words) quote or paraphrase from the source describing what confirmed_spread_value measures.
example_value: table comparing NADAC acquisition cost to price plan agreed to pay (sole-candidate)

description: Category of source_name: federal_study, state_disclosure, peer_reviewed, or litigation. See leaderboard.csv's source_type for the full definitions and the selection-bias caveat.

Blank for nearly every row. Flags a known internal inconsistency in the underlying source itself (not an extraction error on our part) -- e.g. the source states two different figures for the same drug in two places. Where present, this text is appended to leaderboard.csv's best_confirmed_source as a [NOTE: ...] suffix. See METHODOLOGY.md "Technical Validation."
example_value: Complaint's own p.38 narrative computes 1,420.7% for the identical dollar figures ($876.60 NADAC, $13,325.83 price) used in this p.44 exhibit table's 1,395.60%. Cited as printed on p.44; source's internal inconsistency, not corrected.
